body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages` **Service directory:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` **Triggered by:** d2dee26410b69500a2533cdae873a2ec18655541 (PR #56911) by @MaiLinhP ## What Changed PR #56911 migrated `Azure.Communication.Messages` to the new TypeSpec emitter. As part of this migration, two new public client classes (`ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`) are now present in the package as part of the Unified Messaging feature (introduced in v1.3.0-beta.1). Additionally, message types added in v1.1.0 through v1.3.0-beta.2 (document/video/audio notification content, interactive messages, reaction messages, sticker messages, read receipts, typing indicators) remain undocumented in the README. ## Gaps Found - `ConversationAdministrationClient` is a public class with no documentation in README — not mentioned in Key concepts, no usage examples, and no authentication guidance - `ConversationThreadClient` is a public class (authenticated via `CommunicationTokenCredential`) with no documentation in README — not mentioned in Key concepts and no usage examples - "Key concepts" section only lists `NotificationMessagesClient` and is missing the two Conversation clients - `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, and `AudioNotificationContent` added in v1.1.0 have no examples (only `ImageNotificationContent` is shown) - Interactive Message, Reaction Message, and Sticker Message types added in v1.2.0-beta.1 are not documented - Read receipt and typing indicator (v1.3.0-beta.2) are not documented - "Using statements" and "Authenticate the client" subsections are placed under "Key concepts" instead of under "Getting started" as required by the standard README template
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` — **edit** 1. Move the "Using statements" and "Authenticate the client" content from "Key concepts" into "Getting started", after the "Prerequisites" subsection. 2. Expand the "Key concepts" section to describe all four main clients: - `NotificationMessagesClient` — sends one-way notification messages (WhatsApp / SMS) - `MessageTemplateClient` — retrieves message templates - `ConversationAdministrationClient` — creates and manages conversation threads (service-side, uses connection string or `AzureKeyCredential`) - `ConversationThreadClient` — sends and receives messages within a conversation thread (participant-side, uses `CommunicationTokenCredential`) 3. Add an "Authenticate the client" subsection for the Conversation clients. Example: ```C# Snippet:Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationAdministrationClientCreate var connectionString = ""; ConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(connectionString); ``` ```C# Snippet:Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationThreadClientCreate var endpoint = new Uri(""); var communicationTokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(""); ConversationThreadClient threadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(endpoint, communicationTokenCredential); ``` 4. Add an "Examples" subsection for Unified Messaging (conversation operations), e.g., listing conversations and sending a conversation message. 5. Add examples for the media types missing from README: `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, `AudioNotificationContent`. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The repository uses `Update-Snippets.ps1` to inject code from test files into README placeholders. Snippets should be written in a test file and referenced in the README. **Snippet test file path:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationClients.cs` Create the test file with sample methods for: - Creating a `ConversationAdministrationClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationAdministrationClientCreate`) - Creating a `ConversationThreadClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationThreadClientCreate`) Wrap each block with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. Use `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` for test-only setup/cleanup. Reference `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as a canonical example. In the README, add snippet placeholders at the appropriate locations: ``` ```C# Snippet:Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationAdministrationClientCreate ``` (empty body — `Update-Snippets.ps1` fills this in) ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` must contain these sections in this order. The following are currently missing or misplaced: 1. **Getting started** - Install the package ✅ - Prerequisites ✅ - Authenticate the client — currently split between "Getting started" (only for NotificationMessagesClient/MessageTemplateClient) and "Key concepts" (Using statements). Consolidate here and add auth examples for `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`. 2. **Key concepts** — needs expansion (see above) 3. **Examples** — needs new subsections for Conversation clients and additional media types 4. **Troubleshooting** ✅ 5. **Next steps** ✅ 6. **Contributing** ✅ Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` — check git diff to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 communication` — if any public types or members changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Communication Messages] Docs: Add Unified Messaging client documentation to README
